title: User-Installed Software Detections on Corporate Images

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 CM-11 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.

app: Microsoft SCCM / Intune logs via HEC, Jamf logs
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype=intune:device_inventory earliest=-7d
| search publisher!="Microsoft Corporation"
| lookup approved_software_catalog.csv name OUTPUT allowed
| where allowed!="true" OR isnull(allowed)
description: Lists unauthorized software packages reported by inventory agents for CM-11 user-installed software.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes endpoint and sourcetypes intune:device_inventory, intune:device_inventory`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Curate catalog with security exceptions; (2) version-aware matching; (3) self-service remediation tickets; (4) integrate with software blocklist. Indexes required: `index=endpoint`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=intune:device_inventory`, `sourcetype=intune:device_inventory``; Lookups: `approved_software_catalog`, `approved_software_catalog.csv`.
visualization: Table (unapproved titles), Bar chart (by publisher), Single value (devices affected)
knownFalsePositives: Config changes during scheduled maintenance, controlled deployments, or platform upgrades.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Microsoft SCCM / Intune logs via HEC, Jamf logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=intune:device_inventory`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Curate catalog with security exceptions; (2) version-aware matching; (3) self-service remediation tickets; (4) integrate with software blocklist.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype=intune:device_inventory earliest=-7d
| search publisher!="Microsoft Corporation"
| lookup approved_software_catalog.csv name OUTPUT allowed
| where allowed!="true" OR isnull(allowed)
```

Understanding this SPL

**User-Installed Software Detections on Corporate Images (CM-11)** — Lists unauthorized software packages reported by inventory agents for CM-11 user-installed software.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=intune:device_inventory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Microsoft SCCM / Intune logs via HEC, Jamf logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: endpoint; **sourcetype**: intune:device_inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=endpoint, sourcetype=intune:device_inventory, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where allowed!="true" OR isnull(allowed)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unapproved titles), Bar chart (by publisher), Single value (devices affected)
grandmaExplanation: We lists unauthorized software packages reported by inventory agents for CM-11 user-installed software. That helps federal and agency security reviews match what the systems actually do day to day.
